channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Virginia residents outsmarted home invaders, stole their getaway car: prosecutor
item.description: Dogs really are a man's best friend – In Norfolk, Virginia one even provided a distraction that allowed a home invasion victim to escape.
item.dc:creator: Kenzie Finch, KRON4

item.title: Senate Republicans vow action to boost border funds after briefing with Trump officials
item.description: Senate Republicans are vowing to move quickly on a budget plan that could pave the way for about $340 billion in additional border security and defense spending.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Freking And Lisa Mascaro, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Canned tuna sold at major grocers recalled due to food poisoning risk
item.description: LOS ANGELES (KTLA) - Several brands of canned tuna sold at major stores, including Trader Joe's, Costco, and H-E-B, have been recalled due to possible contamination. Tri-Union Seafoods, based in El Segundo, California, announced the recall last week.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: A pilot on a private jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer killed in Arizona aircraft collision
item.description: SCOTTSDALE, Ariz. (AP) — One of two pilots on a private jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer Vince Neil died after the aircraft veered off a runway in Arizona and hit a business jet, according to a Federal Aviation Administration incident report.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pope rebukes Trump administration over migrant deportations, and appears to take direct aim at Vance
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis issued a major rebuke Tuesday to the Trump administration’s plans for mass deportations of migrants, warning that the forceful removal of people purely because of their illegal status deprives them of their inherent dignity and “will end badly.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: What to know about the Trump administration moving to drop corruption charges against NYC mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams was thrown a lifeline when the Trump administration moved to drop federal corruption charges against the embattled leader of America’s largest city.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man linked to Arizona teen Alicia Navarro sentenced to prison for child sexual abuse images
item.description: BILLINGS, Mont. (AP) — A Montana man who had been living with a teenager who disappeared from her home in Arizona years earlier was sentenced to decades in a prison for possession of child sex abuse material, authorities said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wisconsin's top education official facing challengers on both flanks
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — The race to become Wisconsin’s top education official pits the Democratic-backed incumbent against a school choice advocate aligned with Republicans and a rural school superintendent who has garnered support from teachers unions and school administrators.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Survivors of child abuse at Illinois youth detention centers file more lawsuits in hopes of change
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Marcus Walker said the sexual abuse he experienced two decades ago as a teenager at an Illinois youth detention facility has haunted his life. After a staff member gave him drugs and sexually abused him, he joined a gang hoping it would protect him. It didn't.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Video shows speeding jet owned by Mötley Crüe's Vince Neil plow into parked plane
item.description: Dramatic video has been released showing the moment a private jet owned by Vince Neil – the lead singer of Mötley Crüe – veer off a runway in Scottsdale Airport in Arizona and smash into a parked plane.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Good Samaritan helps deputies capture suspect in foot chase in Denver
item.description: “There was a car on its rims, it didn’t have tires and there was a lot of fire coming from the rims,” he said.
item.dc:creator: Nate Belt, NewsNation

item.title: Pentagon changes Fort Liberty's name back to Fort Bragg
item.description: The Pentagon will change the name of the North Carolina military base Fort Liberty back to Fort Bragg, fulfilling a campaign pledge by President Trump while honoring a different eponym.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: No, your student loans won't be forgiven if DOGE illegally accessed your data
item.description: No, you won't get your student loans forgiven if DOGE violated FERPA, even though TikTok videos claim otherwise.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trader Joe's, Costco among grocers to limit how many eggs customers can buy
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Trader Joe’s and other grocers are limiting the number of eggs customers can buy across the U.S., citing limited supply caused by the ongoing bird flu outbreak. Trader Joe’s is capping purchases to one carton per customer each day, the Monrovia, California-based chain confirmed.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump signs executive order restoring plastic straw use in government
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order directing the federal government to return to using plastic straws.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Crush of Valentine's Day flowers: Nearly a billion go through this city to you
item.description: If any husbands or boyfriends mess up Valentine's Day this week, it's not because of a shortage of flowers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Canned tuna sold at Walmart, Trader Joe's, Costco, others is recalled in multiple states
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has announced a recall on canned tuna products sold at several different retailers including Walmart, Trader Joe's, Kroger, and Costco.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Private jets collide at Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, killing 1 person, authorities say
item.description: SCOTTSDALE, Ariz. (AP) — One person was killed and others were injured when a private jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer Vince Neil collided with another jet Monday afternoon at the Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trader Joe's, Smith's limit daily egg purchases due to nationwide shortages
item.description: Trader Joe's is limiting egg sales to one dozen per customer daily -- and it is not the only company doing so.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, ABC 4

item.title: Trump’s Department of Homeland Security looking to deputize IRS agents for immigration enforcement
item.description: DHS Secretary Kristi Noem sent a letter to U.S. Treasury secretary Scott Bessent, asking that IRS agents be provided to assist with immigration and enforcement efforts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Los Angeles family featured in Super Bowl commercial
item.description: LOS ANGELES (KTLA) – Taco Bell marked its return to the Super Bowl with a commercial that featured nearly 400 fans, including a couple from Los Angeles and their newborn baby.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Trader Joe's is now limiting egg sales nationwide
item.description: Trader Joe’s patrons nationwide have taken to social media to comment on a new policy that limits the amount of eggs shoppers can buy. The company told Nexstar's KOIN that limits are currently in place at all of its U.S. locations.
item.dc:creator: Jashayla Pettigrew, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump has unleashed chaos by distraction upon the international community. That's no accident
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The Saudis are furious. The Danes are scrambling. Colombia has backed down. Mexico and Canada stand in a purgatory between tariff wars with the US and … not. China has retaliated, launching a trade war between the economic superpowers.
item.dc:creator: LAURIE KELLMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Third judge blocks Trump’s order ending birthright citizenship for kids of people in US illegally
item.description: CONCORD, N.H. (AP) — A third federal judge on Monday blocked President Donald Trump’s executive order ending birthright citizenship for the children of people who are in the U.S. illegally. The ruling from U.S. District Judge Joseph N.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 6 hidden messages in Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl performance
item.description: Kendrick Lamar’s Super Bowl halftime performance is growing in viewership hours after the big game, gaining more than 11 million views since it aired Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, WGN 9

item.title: Black educators warn of what is lost if Education Department is shuttered
item.description: Black educators are sounding the alarm as President Donald Trump ramps up talk of eliminating the Department of Education.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, Fox 5

item.title: Trump tells Treasury Department to stop making pennies
item.description: President Trump claims he has instructed the U.S. Treasury Department to stop producing pennies, calling the 1-cent coin wasteful.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Senate Democrats launch portal for whistleblowers in Trump administration
item.description: Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.) and Sen. Gary Peters (D-Mich.), the ranking member of the Senate Homeland Security Committee, said they are launching a new portal for whistleblowers to expose wrongdoing and abuses of power in the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Eagles deny the Chiefs a Super Bowl three-peat with dominant defense in a 40-22 rout
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Yo Philly: Jalen Hurts and Nick Sirianni are world champions. A ferocious Philadelphia Eagles defense tormented and frustrated Patrick Mahomes while Hurts made all the plays the offense needed. So much for the Kansas City Chiefs’ quest for a Super Bowl three-peat.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Is the Super Bowl halftime singer lip syncing? Check for these signs
item.description: Casual fans will be watching the theatrics, choreography and guest appearances during Kendrick Lamar and SZA's Super Bowl halftime show. Sleuths will be watching their throats.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Eagles beat Chiefs 40-22 in lopsided Super Bowl LIX
item.description: The Kansas City Chiefs wanted to make history with three back-to-back-to-back championships, but Super Bowl LIX was dominated by the Philadelphia Eagles, who won the game 40-22.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says 25 percent tariffs on steel and aluminum coming Monday
item.description: Trump said that the steel and aluminum tariffs would impact “everybody” when asked what countries would be affected.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KRON4

item.title: Trump administration orders consumer protection agency to stop work, closes building
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has ordered the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau to stop nearly all its work, effectively shutting down an agency that was created to protect consumers after the 2008 financial crisis and subprime mortgage-lending scandal.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER RUGABER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The remains of all 10 people killed in Alaska plane crash have been recovered, authorities say
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — The remains of all 10 people killed when their small plane crashed into ice on the Bering Sea have been recovered, authorities said. The Nome Volunteer Fire Department made the announcement on its Facebook page Saturday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ohio city files federal lawsuit against alleged neo-Nazi group
item.description: The city of Springfield, Ohio has filed a lawsuit against an alleged neo-Nazi group for conducting what it claims to be a campaign of threats and intimidation.
item.dc:creator: LeAnne Marie McPherson, ABC 4

item.title: US skier Breezy Johnson marks return from 14-month ban with a gold in world championship downhill
item.description: Her top speed of 86 mph (138.51 kph) was among the fastest ever recorded by a woman in an Alpine skiing race.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DAMPF, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: A hidden gem of whitewater rafting faces an uncertain future after Hurricane Helene
item.description: ERWIN, Tenn. (AP) — After 24 years of guiding whitewater trips on the Nolichucky River Gorge for other companies, Patrick Mannion finally received a permit last year to operate his own outfitter business.
item.dc:creator: TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, ABC 4
